title: Regime-specific blending with beta calibration on OOF logits
summary: Public calibration work suggests isotonic can overfit smaller slices, while beta calibration is a lighter fix for skewed score distributions.
rationale: Public calibration work suggests isotonic can overfit smaller slices, while beta calibration is a lighter fix for skewed score distributions. In this competition, different regimes likely behave differently: wet vs slick, early vs late race, and first stint vs later stints.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 diverse branches such as current feature-heavy LightGBM/XGBoost, CatBoost categorical branch, and hazard/sequence branch. Produce strictly OOF logits, then fit a simple blender whose weights depend on coarse regimes (`Is_WetWeather`, `RaceProgress` band, `Stint`, `LapsRemaining_Est`). Apply beta calibration per broad regime or on final blended logits only if calibration improves OOF AUC/logloss without slice overfit.
expected_effect: More robust public LB transfer by letting different models dominate where they are structurally strongest.
risk: AUC is rank-based, so over-calibration can be neutral or harmful; regime splitting must stay coarse to avoid fitting noise.
